text: When designing REST APIs, following best practices ensures reliability and ease of use. First, use clear, consistent endpoints that represent resources, like '/users' or '/orders', avoid vague names. Proper status codes are essential - for example, 200 for success, 201 when a resource is created, and 404 if not found. Always include documentation that explains request formats, responses, and examples, so developers understand how to integrate. Finally, versioning is crucial; include a version number, such as '/v1/', in your URL path to handle changes gracefully without breaking existing integrations. These practices create robust, developer-friendly APIs.
